source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A spring is hung vertically from a fixed point. A mass M is hung from the other end of the spring, as illustrated in Fig. 3.1. The mass is displaced downwards and then released. The subsequent motion of the mass is simple harmonic. The variation with time $t$ of the length $L$ of the spring is shown in Fig. 3.2. (a) State: (ii) one time at which the spring has maximum elastic potential energy.
extraction_notes: images field contains PIL image object string representations rather than explicit file paths or URLs

image_dependency: Question explicitly references Fig. 3.1 and Fig. 3.2 and asks for a time read from the plotted variation of spring length with time.
Input marks `requires_image=true` and `text_dominant=false`, indicating the figure is central rather than optional.
The needed evidence is visual/graphical: identifying when the spring length is maximal from the time graph.
multi_step: Solving requires linking the physics concept 'maximum elastic potential energy' to the spring being at maximum extension/length, then locating corresponding time(s) on the graph.
The answer allows multiple valid times (`0`, `0.40 s`, `0.80 s`), so the solver must interpret repeated extrema rather than extract a single literal label.
The task is still relatively short and localized, so the reasoning depth is moderate rather than high.
verifiability: Answer format is constrained short text with a small finite set of acceptable responses: `0`, `0.40 s`, or `0.80 s`.
The target can be checked against the graph extrema and against the provided accepted answers.
Asset registry passed with no issues (`registry_passed=true`), and both question and answer are present.
